steps.title: Set equations equal and expand to standard form
steps.content:
Setting the two expressions for $y$ equal to find intersection points:
$$x - c = -6(x-12)^2$$
Expand $(x-12)^2 = x^2 - 24x + 144$:
$$x - c = -6x^2 + 144x - 864$$
Rearrange to standard quadratic form:
$$6x^2 - 143x + (864 - c) = 0$$

steps.title: Apply discriminant condition for two distinct solutions
steps.content:
For this quadratic to have two distinct real solutions, the discriminant must be positive: $\Delta = b^2 - 4ac > 0$.
$$(-143)^2 - 4(6)(864-c) > 0$$
$$20449 - 24(864-c) > 0$$
$$20449 - 20736 + 24c > 0$$
$$24c > 287$$
$$c > \frac{287}{24}$$

steps.title: Check which answer satisfies the constraint
steps.content:
We need $c > \frac{287}{24}$. Since $\frac{287}{24} = 11\frac{23}{24} \approx 11.96$, check each answer:
  • A) $7 < 11.96$ ✗
  • B) $11 < 11.96$ ✗
  • C) $\frac{287}{24}$ is the boundary (gives exactly one solution) ✗
  • D) $24 > 11.96$ ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
